title: Atitara inermis
taxonomy: Atitara inermis is classified within the family Apocynaceae and the genus Atitara. Atitara inermis is native to tropical Africa, specifically in regions of Cameroon and the Democratic Republic of the Congo. It belongs to the order Gentianales, which comprises several families of flowering plants. The species name inermis suggests that Atitara inermis is unarmed or lacks thorns, possibly distinguishing it from other plants within the same genus. Atitara inermis is a part of the larger taxonomic group known as angiosperms, or flowering plants.

intro: Atitara inermis, also known as Kalmegh or Mexican prickly poppy, is a plant species belonging to the family Apiaceae. It is traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and digestive problems, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and carminative properties. The bioactive constituents of Atitara inermis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In Ayurvedic medicine, Kalmegh is commonly used to prepare herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and powders, which are often combined with other herbs to enhance their therapeutic effects.
